title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Behavior Case: Stable Route Identity
summary: Titles become unstable routes, Unicode collapses into empty slugs, case-only paths collide on deployment, old links vanish after rename, and canonical URLs point at preview origins. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stable route identity increment in collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.
Turn missing templates, undefined variables, invalid blocks, unsafe values, and source locations into actionable build failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_slugs_permalinks_5(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"

For repair the stable route identity defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Stable Route Identity model · Theory Stable Route Iden…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Stable Route Identity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 2506 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Stable Route Identity model · Theory Stable Route Iden….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Stable Route Identity model · Theory Stable Route Iden…, keep Stable Route Identity connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Stable Route Identity model · Theory Stable Route Iden…, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Stable Route Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Stable Route Identity model · Theory Stable Route Iden…, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 2506 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Stable Route Identity model · Theory Stable Route Iden…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: The page title is the best permanent URL identifier.
